title: rosidae
intro: The rank Rosidae, also known as Rosids, is a clade of flowering plants that share a common ancestor and distinct characteristics. It is one of the two main groups of flowering plants, the other being the Asteridae. Examples of orders within the Rosidae clade include Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales, which encompass a diverse range of plants such as orchids, willows, and grasses. The Rosidae clade is characterized by the presence of a specific type of fruit, known as an aggregate fruit, and a variety of growth habits.

orders.order_desc: Malpighiales is a clade within the subclass Rosidae, class Magnoliopsida, subclass Magnoliidae, class Magnoliopsida, division Magnoliophyta, kingdom Plantae.
Fabales is a suborder of the order Fabales, class Magnoliopsida, subclass Rosidae, division Magnoliophyta, phylum Tracheophyta, kingdom Plantae.
Oxalidales is an order of flowering plants, classified within the clade Rosidae.
Rosales is a large order of flowering plants that includes roses, plums, and other related families such as Rosaceae, Fabaceae, and Umatrellaceae.
Sapindales is a plant order within the clade rosids, which is part of the clade eurosids I in the class Magnoliopsida of the kingdom Plantae.
Geraniales belong to the classification of Magnoliophyta, a division of flowering plants.
Saxifragales is an order of flowering plants that belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, subclass Dilleniidae, division Magnoliophyta, and kingdom Plantae.

Oleales is a subfamily of flowering plants that belongs to the order Lamiales.
